$comment: c95 (Windows-host sub-arc, FLIP-AND-UNBLOCK): the pe-gated `_WIN32`/`_WIN64` predefined macros + the `__stdcall`/`WINAPI` erase + the shipped windows.json descriptor + the quote->angle include fallback, exercised end to end. The pe target predefines `_WIN32`, so `#if defined(_WIN32)` selects the Windows branch: it quote-`#include "windows.h"` (resolved to windows.json via the C 6.10.2p3 quote->angle fallback), declares a `DWORD __stdcall` function (parses only because `__stdcall` ERASES on pe), uses a HANDLE + the MAX_PATH constant, and assembles exit 42. Every non-pe target has NO `_WIN32` (the macro is availableObjectFormats:[pe]) so it compiles the `#else` branch — proving the mechanism is per-format-gated with no elf/macho/arm64 regression (the guarded include is skipped, so windows.json's pe-only availability is never hit off-pe). All four running targets share exit 42 (the manifest carries one exitCode); the pe route DERIVES it from windows.h facts while the else route returns it directly. RED-ON-DISABLE: removing `_WIN32` from c-subset.lang.json's predefinedMacros flips the pe target onto the else path AND breaks the (now-unguarded-on-pe) windows.h resolution — witnessed in the c95 scratchpad (red_on_disable.txt).
